name: Post Matric Scholarship for SC Students (NSP)
description: Central government scholarship for Scheduled Caste students pursuing post-matric education. Covers tuition, maintenance allowance, and other charges for SC students from economically weaker families.
eligibility_criteria: SC category, family income below 2.5 lakh per annum, enrolled in post-matric course

name: Post Matric Scholarship for ST Students (NSP)
description: Central government scholarship for Scheduled Tribe students pursuing post-matric education. Provides financial assistance for tuition and living expenses to ST students from lower income households.
eligibility_criteria: ST category, family income below 2.5 lakh per annum, enrolled in post-matric course

name: Post Matric Scholarship for OBC Students (NSP)
description: National Scholarship Portal scholarship for Other Backward Class students. Supports OBC students pursuing higher education with financial assistance for academic and living expenses.
eligibility_criteria: OBC category, family income below 3 lakh per annum, enrolled in post-matric course

name: Central Sector Interest Subsidy Scheme (CSIS)
description: CSIS provides full interest subsidy on education loans during moratorium period for students from economically weaker sections. Covers interest on loans up to 7.5 lakh for technical and professional courses.
eligibility_criteria: Family income below 4.5 lakh, education loan from scheduled bank, enrolled in technical/professional course

name: Prerana Scholarship — Odisha SC/ST
source: Odisha ST and SC Development Department
description: Odisha state scholarship for SC and ST students pursuing higher education within the state. Provides financial aid for tuition and hostel charges to encourage tribal and Dalit students.
eligibility_criteria: SC/ST category, Odisha domicile, family income below 2 lakh, minimum 45% marks

name: Medhabruti Scholarship — Odisha
description: Merit-based scholarship for Odisha students who have scored 60% or above in their qualifying examination. Encourages academic excellence among students of all categories in Odisha.
eligibility_criteria: Odisha domicile, minimum 60% in qualifying exam, enrolled in graduation or post-graduation

name: Merit Cum Means Scholarship for Minority Students
description: Scholarship for students belonging to six minority communities — Muslim, Christian, Sikh, Buddhist, Zoroastrian, and Jain. Covers maintenance and tuition for post-matric studies on merit-cum-means basis.
eligibility_criteria: Minority community, family income below 2.5 lakh, minimum 50% marks, enrolled in graduation or post-graduation

name: INSPIRE Scholarship (DST)
description: Innovation in Science Pursuit for Inspired Research (INSPIRE) scholarship for students pursuing natural and basic sciences at undergraduate and post-graduate level. Aims to attract talent to science research.
eligibility_criteria: Enrolled in natural/basic sciences, scored in top 1% in class 12 board exams or NTS/KVPY qualified

name: Pragati Scholarship — AICTE (Girls in Technical Education)
description: AICTE Pragati scholarship for girl students enrolled in technical degree or diploma programmes. One girl per family can avail per year. Covers tuition, living expenses for 30,000 beneficiaries annually.
eligibility_criteria: Female, enrolled in AICTE-approved degree/diploma, family income below 8 lakh, one per family

name: Saksham Scholarship — AICTE (Persons with Disability)
description: AICTE Saksham scholarship for students with disability (40% or more) enrolled in degree or diploma courses. Promotes inclusive education for differently-abled students in technical fields.
eligibility_criteria: Minimum 40% disability certificate, enrolled in AICTE-approved degree/diploma, family income below 8 lakh

name: NBCFDC Educational Loan Subsidy Scheme
source: National Backward Classes Finance and Development Corporation
description: NBCFDC provides education loan subsidy for OBC and EBC students pursuing technical and professional courses. Helps backward class students access higher education through subsidised financing.
eligibility_criteria: OBC/EBC category, family income below 3 lakh, enrolled in technical or professional course

name: EWS Scholarship Odisha
description: Odisha scholarship for Economically Weaker Section students who do not fall under SC/ST/OBC reservation. Supports EWS families in funding their children's higher education in Odisha.
eligibility_criteria: EWS category, Odisha domicile, family income below 2 lakh, minimum 45% marks

name: Biju Patnaik Merit Scholarship — Odisha
description: Named after Biju Patnaik, this merit scholarship for Odisha students supports meritorious students from lower income families. Encourages academic excellence and higher education within Odisha.
eligibility_criteria: Odisha domicile, family income below 3.5 lakh, minimum 65% in qualifying exam

name: Santali Medium Students Scholarship — Odisha
source: Odisha ST and SC Development Department
description: Special scholarship for tribal students from Santali medium schools in Odisha. Supports ST students who studied in Santali/tribal medium schools to pursue higher education and preserve tribal heritage.
eligibility_criteria: ST category, Odisha domicile, studied in Santali medium school, family income below 1.5 lakh

name: SC/ST Girls Hostel Scholarship
source: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment
description: Scholarship covering hostel and accommodation expenses for SC/ST girl students residing in hostels while pursuing higher education. Aims to reduce barriers for Dalit and tribal women accessing education.
eligibility_criteria: SC/ST category, female, residing in hostel, family income below 2 lakh, enrolled in graduation or post-graduation

name: Vidya Lakshmi Education Loan Portal Scholarship
source: Ministry of Education / Vidya Lakshmi Portal
description: Interest subsidy and scholarship component under the Vidya Lakshmi portal for students applying for education loans. Provides a unified interface for scholarship and loan applications from multiple banks.
eligibility_criteria: Family income below 4.5 lakh, applied through Vidya Lakshmi portal, enrolled in recognized institution

name: CSIS Interest Subsidy for Technical Courses
source: Ministry of Education / IBA Model Scheme
description: Variant of the Central Sector Interest Subsidy for students in technical and professional courses. Covers interest during moratorium period for students from EWS families enrolled in IBA-approved courses.
eligibility_criteria: Family income below 4.5 lakh, enrolled in IBA-approved technical/professional course, education loan from scheduled bank

name: Dr. Ambedkar Post-Matric Fellowship
source: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment
description: Fellowship named after Dr. B.R. Ambedkar for SC and OBC students pursuing higher education. Provides comprehensive financial support for post-matric students to help break cycles of educational disadvantage.
eligibility_criteria: SC/OBC category, family income below 2.5 lakh, enrolled in post-matric course, minimum 50% marks

name: HDFC Educational Crisis Scholarship Trust
description: One-time crisis scholarship for students facing sudden financial hardship due to loss of parent or guardian income. HDFC Bank helps students continue their education during family financial emergencies.
eligibility_criteria: Any category, family income below 5 lakh, facing financial crisis (loss of parent/guardian), minimum 55% marks

name: Tata Capital Pankh Scholarship
description: Annual scholarship by Tata Capital for deserving students from economically weaker sections. The Pankh (wings) scholarship supports students pursuing graduation or diploma courses with comprehensive financial assistance.
eligibility_criteria: Any category, family income below 4 lakh, minimum 50% marks, enrolled in graduation or diploma course
